unit.name: Compounding & Mixing
unit.cfj: Dissolve, blend, and adjust active and excipient components into homogeneous bulk solution at target concentration

meta.method: Ulwick ODI (Importance × Satisfaction → Opportunity), needs scoring v2

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of declaring batch endpoint while the solution is not yet homogeneous at target concentration
rows.imp_rat: Declaring endpoint before true homogeneity at target concentration is the worst failure of this job, sending an out-of-spec bulk downstream to fill-finish.

rows.stmt: Minimize the percentage of procedural deviations that go undetected until after the batch is compounded
rows.imp_rat: Deviations detected only after compounding leave the supervisor with an unrecoverable batch, so early detection is critical to the adherence job.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that compounding parameters validated at small scale fail to reproduce homogeneity at production scale
rows.imp_rat: Scale-up parameters that fail to reproduce homogeneity at production scale directly negate the scientist's core recommendation and cause costly tech-transfer failures.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of uncertainty in quantifying the financial exposure tied to the compounding-capability commitment
rows.imp_rat: Quantifying financial exposure on the compounding commitment is central to defensible risk-adjusted authorization and to protecting the site's capital.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood that acceptance criteria pass a bulk solution that is actually non-uniform
rows.imp_rat: Criteria that pass an actually non-uniform bulk is a patient-safety escape and the worst possible failure of the homogeneity criteria the scientist sets.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of microbial or particulate ingress into the bulk during charging and mixing operations
rows.imp_rat: Microbial or particulate ingress during the open charging step is the defining sterility risk this operator must prevent to preserve batch integrity.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the compounding process drifting outside its validated parameters undetected over time
rows.imp_rat: Undetected drift outside validated compounding parameters is precisely the loss of validated state the QA manager is charged to govern.

rows.stmt: Minimize the amount of finite capital diverted to a lower-value priority at the expense of the highest-return compounding investment option
rows.imp_rat: As the authorizer of finite site capital, misdirecting funds away from the highest-return compounding option directly undermines the core allocation job.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of recurring deviations that repeat because a prior corrective action failed to address the root cause in the compounding operation
rows.imp_rat: Recurring deviations from ineffective CAPA signal systemic failure and repeatedly draw inspector attention to the QA manager's system.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood that a mixing approach proven at bench scale fails to hold homogeneity within full production-scale constraints
rows.imp_rat: Bench-to-production scale-up failure of homogeneity is the classic, high-stakes risk this engineer is specifically accountable for preventing.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of releasing a compounded batch that later proves out of specification on a retained sample
rows.imp_rat: Releasing a batch that later fails a retained sample is a worst-case disposition error triggering recall and regulatory exposure the QA manager owns.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the bulk solution deviating from target concentration specification at release
rows.imp_rat: Assuring the bulk hits target concentration at release is the ultimate purpose of the recommended conditions, and a deviation is a batch-level and regulatory failure.

rows.stmt: Minimize the number of batch records reaching disposition with incomplete or conflicting data that obscures whether specification was met
rows.imp_rat: Incomplete or conflicting batch records obscure whether specification was met, directly impairing the QA manager's disposition decision.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time a validated parameter remains out of its qualified range before the condition is recognized and addressed
rows.imp_rat: The dwell time a parameter sits out-of-range before recognition determines batch impact scope, a key concern for the QA manager governing validated state.

rows.stmt: Minimize the number of compliance gaps in the compounding operation that surface only during a regulatory inspection
rows.imp_rat: Compliance gaps surfacing only during inspection represent oversight failures that fall squarely on the QA manager's regulatory-readiness duty.

rows.stmt: Minimize the amount of undissolved solid particulate remaining in the bulk solution at the end of the dissolution step
rows.imp_rat: Achieving full dissolution is the operator's core deliverable, and residual undissolved solids directly mean the bulk fails its concentration and clarity requirements.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of aseptic technique lapses by the operator during open compounding manipulations
rows.imp_rat: An aseptic technique lapse during open charging can contaminate the entire sterile bulk, which is the highest-stakes failure mode in this operator's job.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of excursions outside the specified tolerance range during the run
rows.imp_rat: Holding speed, temperature and pH inside tolerance for the whole run is the literal core of this technician's job and any excursion risks a non-conforming sterile bulk.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of charging raw components in the wrong sequence when starting the batch
rows.imp_rat: Charging components out of sequence can irreversibly ruin a sterile bulk and trigger a deviation, making order-of-addition control critical at setup.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of introducing contamination while charging components into the mixing vessel
rows.imp_rat: In sterile fill-finish, introducing contamination while charging is a catastrophic outcome the technician must prevent, second only to getting concentration right.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a procedural step being skipped or performed out of order during a compounding run
rows.imp_rat: A skipped or out-of-order compounding step can fail the batch and trigger a deviation, striking at the heart of procedural adherence the supervisor owns.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a nonconforming raw material entering the compounding process undetected against defined acceptance criteria
rows.imp_rat: A nonconforming raw material entering compounding directly threatens sterility and specification of the bulk solution the QA manager must govern.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of batches that miss target concentration despite recommended parameters being followed correctly
rows.imp_rat: Batches missing target despite correctly followed parameters implies the scientist's recommendation itself is flawed, the most damaging outcome for this role.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of incomplete solubilization of components into the bulk solution under the recommended conditions
rows.imp_rat: Incomplete solubilization under the recommended conditions is a direct indictment of the dissolution conditions this scientist defines and can compromise the entire bulk.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of unmanaged changes that alter the validated state of the compounding process without revalidation
rows.imp_rat: Unmanaged changes altering the validated compounding state without revalidation are a direct breach of the QA manager's govern-validated-state job.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a compliance failure that erodes regulators' and leadership's trust in the role's oversight of compounding quality
rows.imp_rat: A compliance failure eroding regulators' and leadership's trust strikes at the QA manager's status as guardian of compounding quality.

rows.stmt: Prevent the likelihood of omitting a critical requirement needed to achieve homogeneous bulk at target concentration
rows.imp_rat: Omitting a critical requirement in the spec directly defeats the engineer's core deliverable of guaranteeing homogeneous bulk at target concentration.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of ambiguity in the homogeneity acceptance criteria that confirm bulk uniformity
rows.imp_rat: Setting unambiguous homogeneity acceptance criteria is the direct deliverable of this scientist's job, so ambiguity that lets reviewers or operators interpret uniformity differently is a core failure.

rows.stmt: Minimize the number of homogeneity failure modes overlooked when assessing risks to the mixing system's bulk-solution output
rows.imp_rat: Overlooking a homogeneity failure mode in the risk assessment is the foundational error that propagates through every downstream qualification step.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a gap between the qualification approach and applicable regulatory expectations going unnoticed
rows.imp_rat: An unnoticed gap between the qualification approach and regulatory expectations is exactly what triggers 483s, making this central to the engineer's compliance job.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of qualifying a supplier that cannot meet the sterility and homogeneity requirements of the compounding unit
rows.imp_rat: Qualifying a supplier that cannot meet sterility and homogeneity requirements is a catastrophic sourcing failure in sterile fill-finish, so preventing it is paramount.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of uncertainty about whether a given manipulation breached aseptic conditions during compounding
rows.imp_rat: In sterile fill-finish an uncertain aseptic manipulation forces conservative reject/investigate decisions, so ambiguity about a breach is costly to the operator's job.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of being surprised by an unknown open issue in the compounding operation during an unannounced inspection
rows.imp_rat: Being blindsided by an unknown open issue during an unannounced inspection is the QA manager's central audit-readiness fear.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty about whether a given mixing capability can reach target homogeneity for a specific formulation viscosity and solids load
rows.imp_rat: Judging whether a mixer can reach target homogeneity for a specific viscosity and solids load is the go/no-go question in assessing equipment capability.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in translating homogeneity and concentration targets into measurable equipment requirements such as tip speed, mixing energy, and vessel geometry
rows.imp_rat: Translating homogeneity and concentration targets into measurable parameters like tip speed and mixing energy is the technical heart of defining equipment requirements.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of misattributing a homogeneity failure to the wrong contributing factor among shear, time, temperature, and addition order
rows.imp_rat: Correctly attributing a homogeneity failure among shear, time, temperature, and addition order is central to resolving mixing deficiencies without chasing the wrong lever.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of uncertainty about which compounding parameters to adjust when feedstock attributes shift between lots
rows.imp_rat: Recommending which compounding parameters to adjust when feedstock shifts between lots is central to keeping the bulk on target, and uncertainty here directly undermines the scientist's guidance.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty in how mixing energy and time translate when batch volume increases
rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty in how mixing energy and time translate with increased volume is the crux of the scale-up recommendation this scientist must defend.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of omitting hidden lifecycle costs such as cleaning validation and consumables from the capital justification for the mixing system
rows.imp_rat: Omitting cleaning-validation and consumables from the capital case is a classic and costly miss for sterile compounding equipment, undermining the financial case this manager owns.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time-to-payback erosion caused by slower-than-planned ramp of the funded compounding capability
rows.imp_rat: Slower-than-planned ramp of compounding capability erodes payback and directly threatens the return the director committed to when authorizing funds.

rows.source_job: feel confident the investment will pay off
rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of doubt about the credibility of the return projection at the moment of authorizing the spend
rows.imp_rat: At the moment of signing, doubt about the credibility of the return projection weighs heavily on the director who is personally accountable for the decision.

rows.source_job: feel reassured the downside is covered
rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of unease about whether the financial downside on this commitment is genuinely contained
rows.imp_rat: Reassurance that the financial downside is genuinely contained is essential emotional cover for committing scarce site capital to a single compounding line.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of personnel performing compounding tasks with a lapsed or unverified qualification status
rows.imp_rat: Allowing unqualified operators to compound is a classic FDA 483 finding and undermines the validated, compliant execution the QA manager certifies.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that a protocol fails to challenge the worst-case mixing condition for homogeneity
rows.imp_rat: A protocol that fails to challenge the worst-case mixing condition produces a qualification that doesn't actually bound real homogeneity risk.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of an out-of-target concentration result going undetected across repeated runs
rows.imp_rat: An undetected out-of-target concentration result across runs means a defective system passes qualification—an unacceptable failure for this role.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of discrepancy between documented procedures and actual compounding practice at any point in time
rows.imp_rat: Divergence between documented procedures and actual compounding practice is a frequent data-integrity and GMP citation the QA manager must prevent.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that consistent homogeneity in test runs fails to predict consistency in routine production
rows.imp_rat: If test-run consistency doesn't predict routine production, the whole reproducibility verification loses meaning to this engineer.

rows.stmt: Minimize the percentage deviation of final bulk concentration from the target value at batch completion
rows.imp_rat: Final bulk concentration deviation from target is the direct measurable output of the operator's core job of holding target concentration.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a component charging-weight error altering the intended concentration of the bulk
rows.imp_rat: A charging-weight error directly shifts bulk concentration off target, striking at the operator's core job of hitting target concentration.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of concentration variation across sampling points within a single bulk batch
rows.imp_rat: Concentration variation across sampling points within a batch is the operational definition of poor homogeneity the scientist is chartered to prevent.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of off-spec or contaminated batches attributed to the operator in quality reviews
rows.imp_rat: Off-spec or contaminated batches attributed to the operator in quality reviews are the most damaging hits to their flawless-compounder status.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time between a mixing parameter drifting out of target and the corrective adjustment taking effect during a run
rows.imp_rat: The lag between a parameter drifting out and the correction taking effect directly determines whether the batch stays in spec, central to the adjust job.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of batches completing later than their committed schedule slot
rows.imp_rat: Batches completing later than their committed slot is the definitive failure of the supervisor's schedule-attainment job.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of variation in actual versus planned completion time across batches
rows.imp_rat: Variation in actual vs planned completion time directly undermines the supervisor's mandate to deliver homogeneous bulk on schedule, feeding downstream fill-finish slots.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time lost to extended dissolution or blending holds before a batch reaches target concentration
rows.imp_rat: Extended dissolution or blending holds before reaching target concentration are the most direct schedule threat within the supervisor's own process step.

rows.stmt: Minimize the percentage of disrupted batches that require re-dissolution or re-blending to recover
rows.imp_rat: Re-dissolution or re-blending to recover a disrupted batch costs hours and vessel time the supervisor is accountable for on schedule.

rows.stmt: Minimize the number of batch records containing entries that do not match what was actually performed
rows.imp_rat: Batch records that don't match what was performed create data-integrity findings the supervisor must prevent to close batches cleanly.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of overlooking a concentration gradient or undissolved fraction when judging whether the bulk batch is homogeneous
rows.imp_rat: Missing a concentration gradient or undissolved fraction means declaring a non-homogeneous batch acceptable, a direct failure of the capability-assessment job.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty in the dissolution endpoint that confirms full solubilization has been achieved
rows.imp_rat: A defensible dissolution endpoint confirming full solubilization is a specific deliverable of the dissolution-conditions job and prevents downstream ambiguity.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of underestimating the severity of a risk that could compromise bulk solution homogeneity
rows.imp_rat: Underestimating a severity that could compromise homogeneity leaves a real hazard under-controlled in the qualified system.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that the defined homogeneity limits fail to bound true product concentration variation
rows.imp_rat: Acceptance limits that fail to bound true concentration variation render the defined qualified state invalid, striking at the criteria-definition job.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of narrowing qualification scope to the point of missing a material homogeneity risk
rows.imp_rat: Over-narrowing scope to save effort while missing a material homogeneity risk is a direct failure of the engineer's verification mandate.

rows.source_job: feel reassured the system won't surprise us
rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an unexpected homogeneity excursion appearing once the system is in routine use
rows.imp_rat: An unexpected homogeneity excursion in routine use is the emotional nightmare that proves the qualification didn't predict reality.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a funding request being rejected for insufficient justification of the mixing system investment
rows.imp_rat: Securing funding approval is the gating step of this job, so a rejection for weak justification directly blocks procuring the compounding system.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of misalignment between the funded bulk-solution compounding capacity and the site's stated production strategy during investment appraisal
rows.imp_rat: Funding compounding capacity that mismatches the site's production strategy wastes capital and is precisely what the director must catch during appraisal.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of decisions later judged to have directed funds away from the highest-value, lowest-risk priority
rows.imp_rat: Being retrospectively judged to have misallocated funds threatens the director's standing as a trusted steward of capital, a core reputational stake of the role.

rows.stmt: Minimize the amount of improvement effort spent on low-impact issues while higher-risk quality gaps in the compounding operation remain unaddressed
rows.imp_rat: Directing finite CAPA and improvement resources toward the highest-risk compounding gaps is central to a QA manager's risk-based quality governance mandate.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty about whether every current record and control in the compounding operation would withstand inspection scrutiny at this moment
rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty about whether every current record would withstand scrutiny is the core of the QA manager's feel-audit-ready emotional job.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time to isolate the root cause when a mixing approach fails to reach target homogeneity or full dissolution
rows.imp_rat: Fast root-cause isolation when homogeneity or dissolution fails limits downtime and rejected bulk, a direct concern of resolving mixing deficiencies.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time to detect that raw material lot variability is threatening bulk homogeneity during compounding
rows.imp_rat: Detecting that lot variability is threatening homogeneity during compounding lets the scientist's adaptive guidance act before the batch is lost.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time to identify the root cause of a dissolution or homogeneity deviation during compounding
rows.imp_rat: Rapid root-cause identification of dissolution or homogeneity deviations directly limits batch disposition delays tied to the scientist's conditions.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of misattributing a homogeneity deviation to the wrong contributing parameter
rows.imp_rat: Misattributing a homogeneity deviation to the wrong parameter leads to wrong corrective recommendations, striking at the scientist's diagnostic credibility.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of recurring deviations that persist because the original root cause was never resolved
rows.imp_rat: Recurring deviations from unresolved root cause reflect incomplete diagnosis and repeatedly reopen the scientist's compounding recommendations.

rows.stmt: Minimize the number of gaps in the traceable evidence chain substantiating the qualification conclusion
rows.imp_rat: A broken traceable evidence chain undermines the entire substantiation of the qualification conclusion during audit.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time spent reconciling open deviations before a release or rejection decision can be made on a compounded batch
rows.imp_rat: Reconciling open deviations before disposition directly gates the QA manager's release decision and cycle time.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time required to retrieve a complete and accurate record when requested during a regulatory inspection
rows.imp_rat: Fast retrieval of complete accurate records during inspection is critical to how the QA manager demonstrates control and avoids findings.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of measurement variability introduced when sampling bulk solution during verification runs
rows.imp_rat: Sampling-induced measurement variability directly obscures whether the bulk solution is truly homogeneous, biasing the verification conclusion.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of concentration variation between sampling points across the bulk solution after blending
rows.imp_rat: Concentration variation between sample points is the direct test of the blend homogeneity the operator is responsible for delivering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of unmixed dead zones persisting in the vessel during blending
rows.imp_rat: Persistent dead zones leave portions of the bulk unmixed, defeating the operator's homogeneity objective.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of critical process parameters such as temperature and agitation rate drifting outside their defined operating ranges during mixing
rows.imp_rat: Temperature and agitation staying in range is essential for the operator to maintain the process conditions that ensure proper dissolution and blending.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of compromised connections or seals on the mixing vessel during a compounding cycle
rows.imp_rat: A compromised vessel connection or seal opens a direct contamination path and can breach batch sterility the operator is charged with protecting.

rows.source_job: feel confident the batch is on-spec
rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of the operator releasing a batch unaware that it is off-target concentration
rows.imp_rat: Unknowingly releasing an off-target batch is exactly the failure the operator's confidence-in-spec emotional job guards against.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a stalled or off-track mixing trend going undetected during the run
rows.imp_rat: An undetected stalled or off-track mixing trend can waste an entire high-value sterile batch, so catching it in-run is central to the monitoring job.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood that assigned personnel arrive without current qualification for the scheduled compounding task
rows.imp_rat: An unqualified operator arriving for a scheduled compounding task can halt the batch and create a compliance exposure the supervisor owns.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of variability in how individual operators execute the same compounding task against defined standards
rows.imp_rat: Operator-to-operator variability in executing compounding tasks threatens the reproducibility the QA manager must assure.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of qualification gaps left undocumented in personnel training records at the time of task assignment
rows.imp_rat: Undocumented qualification gaps at task assignment create compliance exposure the QA manager must foreclose.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of lingering doubt that a just-released compounded batch will return as a deviation or recall
rows.imp_rat: Lingering doubt that a released batch will return as a recall reflects the emotional weight the QA manager carries after each disposition.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of batch-to-batch homogeneity variation arising from inconsistent raw material particle size or purity
rows.imp_rat: Batch-to-batch homogeneity variation from raw material particle size or purity undermines the reproducibility the scientist's parameters are meant to guarantee.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of invalid test results caused by execution errors during verification studies
rows.imp_rat: Invalid verification results from execution errors force costly re-runs and jeopardize the qualification timeline the engineer is accountable for.

rows.source_job: feel confident the qualification will hold up
rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of being blindsided by a challenge to the qualification decision that the evidence cannot answer
rows.imp_rat: Being blindsided by a challenge the evidence can't answer directly attacks the engineer's confidence that the qualification will hold.

rows.source_job: feel relief the batch stayed sterile
rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an undetected aseptic slip leaving the operator anxious about a contamination breach after the cycle
rows.imp_rat: An undetected aseptic slip leaves the operator anxious about a latent contamination breach they cannot resolve, a real emotional stake in sterile work.

rows.source_job: feel relief the batch stayed sterile
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty about whether the compounding cycle stayed contamination-free once it is complete
rows.imp_rat: Post-cycle uncertainty about contamination weighs on the operator's relief that the batch stayed sterile, an explicit emotional job.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of a single disruption cascading into stoppages on adjacent batches in the sequence
rows.imp_rat: A single disruption cascading to adjacent batches multiplies the supervisor's recovery burden and schedule loss.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of overstating equipment shear or recirculation capability based on vendor claims rather than verified data during capability assessment
rows.imp_rat: Overstating shear or recirculation capability from vendor claims propagates into an unfit spec, so verified data matters greatly to the engineer's judgment.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of trial-and-error iterations needed to correct a dissolution or blend deficiency
rows.imp_rat: Cutting trial-and-error iterations to fix a blend deficiency conserves scarce batch material and schedule that the engineer must manage.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty in the projected throughput and yield assumptions underpinning the financial case for the compounding and mixing system
rows.imp_rat: Throughput and yield assumptions underpin the whole financial case, so uncertainty there weakens the justification this manager builds.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty in projected maintenance, spare-parts, and cleaning costs over the service life of the mixing system
rows.imp_rat: Uncertain maintenance, spare-parts, and cleaning costs over service life directly threaten the TCO accuracy this manager must optimize.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of underestimating downstream changeover and cleaning-validation costs when comparing compounding system options
rows.imp_rat: Underestimated changeover and cleaning-validation costs distort option comparisons and inflate true TCO this manager must optimize.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time spent reconciling conflicting stakeholder requirements into a single fundable specification for the mixing system
rows.imp_rat: Reconciling conflicting stakeholder requirements into one fundable spec is a core coordination burden this manager must complete to proceed.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that realized operating costs of the compounding system exceed the figures committed in the business case
rows.imp_rat: When realized operating costs overrun the committed business case, this manager's value-realization credibility is directly on the line.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of losing standing as a disciplined capital steward when a compounding system acquisition overruns its committed lifecycle cost
rows.imp_rat: Losing standing over a lifecycle-cost overrun strikes at this manager's core status as a disciplined steward.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of variability in projected operating spend across the compounding asset's service life
rows.imp_rat: Lifecycle operating-spend variability on the compounding asset drives total cost of ownership, which the director must weigh when justifying the spend.

rows.stmt: Minimize the amount of negotiating leverage lost when supplier alternatives for the specialized mixing system are limited
rows.imp_rat: Specialized sterile mixing systems come from a narrow supplier field, so lost negotiating leverage directly hurts this manager's Negotiate Commercial Terms job.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in the acceptance criteria that define a qualified mixing system
rows.imp_rat: Ambiguous acceptance criteria make the definition of a qualified mixing system contestable, directly hitting the criteria-definition job.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood that documented evidence is contradicted by an inconsistent data record
rows.imp_rat: Contradictory data records destroy the credibility of qualification evidence and invite data-integrity findings.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of run-to-run variation in bulk solution homogeneity across repeated qualification runs
rows.imp_rat: Run-to-run homogeneity variation is the very phenomenon reproducibility verification must bound for a defensible qualification.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of revising the requirement set after downstream constraints such as CIP, fill-finish, or scale-up surface late
rows.imp_rat: Rework of the requirement set after CIP, fill-finish, or scale-up constraints surface late costs time and credibility in the engineer's specification role.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of attributing a performance deviation to the wrong root cause
rows.imp_rat: Misattributing a performance deviation sends corrective effort in the wrong direction and can leave a real homogeneity risk unaddressed.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a parameter reading being inaccurate while the operator relies on it during mixing
rows.imp_rat: The operator adjusts the process based on parameter readings, so an inaccurate temperature or agitation value leads to wrong mixing decisions.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of entering an initial parameter value that differs from the value specified for the batch
rows.imp_rat: An initial setpoint that differs from the batch spec propagates errors through the whole run, so accurate parameter entry is a foundational job step.

rows.stmt: Reduce the rate of component degradation caused by overly aggressive dissolution temperature or hold time
rows.imp_rat: Component degradation from aggressive dissolution temperature or hold time compromises product quality that the scientist's conditions are meant to protect.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of unnecessary operator interventions into the critical zone during a compounding cycle
rows.imp_rat: Every intervention into the critical zone is a contamination opportunity, so minimizing unnecessary ones supports the operator's aseptic duty.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time taken to detect a parameter excursion after it begins during a mixing run
rows.imp_rat: The sooner a parameter excursion is detected, the less bulk is exposed to out-of-range conditions, protecting the operator's process control.

rows.source_job: feel confident the batch is on-spec
rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of doubt about whether the batch is fully dissolved and uniform before releasing it forward
rows.imp_rat: The operator needs confidence the batch is dissolved and uniform before releasing it forward, since doubt here directly burdens their emotional job of assurance.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of batches requiring rework that undermine the operator's right-first-time standing with leadership
rows.imp_rat: Rework batches directly damage the operator's right-first-time reputation with leadership, central to their status as a flawless compounder.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of repeated parameter swings requiring successive re-adjustments within a single batch
rows.imp_rat: Repeated parameter swings within one batch signal poor control and add stress and cycle time, directly undermining the technician's drive-to-homogeneity job.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time a scheduled batch waits for an upstream batch to clear shared mixing equipment during the production sequence
rows.imp_rat: Waiting for an upstream batch to clear shared mixing equipment is a direct queue delay the supervisor must sequence around.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of changeover collisions where two batches contend for the same mixing-tank window in the sequence
rows.imp_rat: Changeover collisions for the same mixing tank stall the supervisor's sequence and force rework of the daily plan, a core coordination risk.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of operators interpreting an ambiguous compounding-procedure instruction differently across batches
rows.imp_rat: Operators interpreting an ambiguous instruction differently across batches introduces variation the supervisor must standardize out.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time between a workflow interruption occurring and the compounding run resuming
rows.imp_rat: Time between an interruption and resuming the run directly extends batch duration and threatens the schedule the supervisor commits to.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of batch documentation being returned for correction after initial review
rows.imp_rat: Documentation returned for correction after review delays batch closure and consumes the supervisor's time to reconcile.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a batch failing review in front of production leadership and damaging the overseer's dependable reputation
rows.imp_rat: A batch failing review in front of leadership directly damages the supervisor's dependable-closer reputation they trade on.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of inconsistency in delivering compliant, on-time bulk solution that peers rely on the overseer to maintain
rows.imp_rat: Consistency in delivering compliant, on-time bulk is the reputation peers rely on, central to the supervisor's status.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of unease that a release decision rested on data not fully verified against specification
rows.imp_rat: Unease that a decision rested on unverified data speaks to the QA manager's need for confidence in each specification-based release.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in interpreting which regulatory expectations apply to bulk solution mixing qualification
rows.imp_rat: Ambiguity over which regulations apply to bulk mixing qualification creates rework and compliance exposure the engineer must preempt.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of homogeneity deviations that remain undiagnosed for lack of traceable test data
rows.imp_rat: Deviations left undiagnosed for want of traceable data leave homogeneity risks unresolved and the qualification exposed.

rows.source_job: feel reassured the system won't surprise us
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of unease that a hidden failure mode could disrupt homogeneous output after qualification
rows.imp_rat: Unease about a hidden failure mode disrupting output reflects the engineer's fear that risk assessment was incomplete.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in warranty and performance-guarantee clauses for the compounding and mixing system before contract signature
rows.imp_rat: Ambiguous warranty and performance-guarantee clauses expose this manager to unrecoverable risk if the mixing system underperforms after signature.

rows.stmt: Prevent the likelihood of accepting unfavorable change-order or price-escalation terms buried in the supply agreement for the compounding system
rows.imp_rat: Buried change-order and escalation clauses in a specialized supply agreement can erode the negotiated value this manager is accountable for.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty about whether budget will remain available across the procurement timeline for the compounding system
rows.imp_rat: Budget evaporating across a long sterile-equipment procurement timeline can strand this manager's committed project mid-cycle.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty in verifying a supplier's track record on comparable mixing system installations
rows.imp_rat: Verifying a supplier's track record on comparable sterile mixing installs is central to sourcing a qualified vendor this manager can trust.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of being seen as having procured the wrong mixing system for the unit's needs
rows.imp_rat: Being seen as having procured the wrong compounding system directly damages this manager's standing as a trusted capital steward.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of acquisitions that fail to protect the organization's invested capital and erode trust in the steward's judgment
rows.imp_rat: Acquisitions that fail to protect invested capital erode the trust this manager depends on for future funding authority.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of deviations that surface too late to feel in control of correcting the compounding parameters
rows.imp_rat: Deviations surfacing too late to correct compounding parameters undermines the sense of control that matters emotionally to this scientist.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of uncertainty about whether a corrective parameter change will actually resolve the deviation
rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty about whether a corrective parameter change will actually resolve a deviation weighs on the scientist's confidence in staying ahead of problems.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of acceptance criteria being interpreted inconsistently across incoming material lots
rows.imp_rat: Inconsistent interpretation of acceptance criteria across lots creates uneven material conformance decisions the QA manager governs.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of oversight lapses that cause the compounding team to question the role's standing as the unwavering guardian of quality
rows.imp_rat: Oversight lapses that make the compounding team question the QA manager's authority weaken the standing needed to enforce quality.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of conforming batches rejected by overly tight homogeneity criteria
rows.imp_rat: Overly tight criteria rejecting conforming batches waste good product and signal the scientist mis-set the acceptance thresholds.

rows.source_job: feel confident the qualification will hold up
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of lingering doubt that the gathered evidence will withstand regulatory and audit scrutiny
rows.imp_rat: Lingering doubt about audit-worthiness of evidence is the core emotional load this engineer carries into every review.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a qualification verdict being overturned and eroding standing as the trusted authority
rows.imp_rat: A verdict being overturned publicly erodes the engineer's standing as the trusted qualification authority.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood that a post-qualification field failure publicly undermines the rigor attributed to the role
rows.imp_rat: A post-qualification field failure publicly undermining the role's rigor is a lasting reputational blow this engineer fears.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of lingering doubt that the specified mixing approach will actually deliver homogeneous bulk at target concentration
rows.imp_rat: Lingering doubt that the specified approach will actually deliver homogeneity strikes at the engineer's core professional confidence in the spec.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of a specified mixing approach failing in production and eroding standing with engineering and operations
rows.imp_rat: A production failure of a specified approach directly erodes the engineer's credibility with engineering and operations, a meaningful reputational stake.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of re-optimization cycles required when transferring compounding parameters to a new processing scale
rows.imp_rat: Re-optimization cycles at a new scale add cost and delay to tech transfer, a recurring pain in the scale-up part of this job.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of variation in dissolution completeness across repeat batches of the same formulation
rows.imp_rat: Batch-to-batch dissolution consistency underpins reliable full dissolution across repeated production of the same formulation.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of batches that fail to hit homogeneity at target concentration on the first attempt under the operator's watch
rows.imp_rat: Hitting homogeneity at target on the first attempt under their watch is how this technician earns the reliable batch-master reputation they care about.

rows.stmt: Minimize the number of deviations traced back to the operator that erode leadership's trust in their batches
rows.imp_rat: Deviations traced back to them erode leadership's trust, a status stake the technician actively manages alongside the technical work.

rows.stmt: Reduce the percentage of record entries that are incomplete or illegible at the time of verification
rows.imp_rat: Incomplete or illegible entries at verification force rework and threaten data integrity the supervisor must certify.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a procurement policy violation going undetected before the compounding system purchase order is issued
rows.imp_rat: An undetected procurement-policy violation before PO issuance exposes this manager to audit and rework, central to the compliance duty of the role.

rows.source_job: trust the bulk will meet target
rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of batch-outcome unpredictability that prevents feeling assured the bulk will hit target
rows.imp_rat: Batch-outcome unpredictability that prevents feeling assured reflects the emotional confidence the scientist wants in their own recommendations, meaningful but secondary to functional results.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time taken to diagnose the root cause of an in-process mixing anomaly during compounding
rows.imp_rat: Faster root-cause diagnosis limits batch exposure during an anomaly, but this is reactive troubleshooting rather than the core mixing task.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty about which corrective action will resolve a given mixing anomaly
rows.imp_rat: Knowing which corrective action fixes an anomaly matters for recovery, but is a contingency rather than the primary mixing task.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of sequence reshuffling required when batch priorities change mid-shift across the unit's compounding workload
rows.imp_rat: Mid-shift priority changes forcing sequence reshuffling disrupt the supervisor's coordination and cascade into other batches.

rows.source_job: feel in control of the batch sequence
rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of the overseer learning a batch has fallen behind schedule only late in the run
rows.imp_rat: Learning late that a batch has fallen behind robs the supervisor of the chance to intervene and stay in control.

rows.stmt: Diminish the degree of subjectivity in ranking the likelihood of identified homogeneity risks across reviewers
rows.imp_rat: Consistent risk ranking across reviewers matters for defensible risk-based qualification, but subjectivity is a secondary concern next to catching the risks themselves.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of rebuilding the financial model after engineering revises the compounding system requirements
rows.imp_rat: Rebuilding the financial model each time engineering revises requirements wastes this manager's cycle time on the financial case.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time spent reconciling supplier qualification documentation against regulated procurement requirements for the sterile compounding asset
rows.imp_rat: Reconciling supplier qualification documentation against regulated procurement requirements is a compliance-critical but time-consuming task in sterile asset buys.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in how competing quality, operations, and finance priorities are weighted in the compounding system decision
rows.imp_rat: Ambiguous weighting of quality, operations, and finance priorities makes the compounding decision hard to defend, which this manager must resolve.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of reopening a settled compounding system decision when a stakeholder raises a late objection
rows.imp_rat: Reopening a settled compounding decision because of a late quality or operations objection burns schedule and credibility, so avoiding it matters to this role.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty in measuring whether the installed compounding system delivered its projected throughput and yield benefits
rows.imp_rat: Being able to measure whether projected throughput and yield were delivered is core to validating the investment this manager championed.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time before a shortfall in the mixing system's expected investment value is detected post-installation
rows.imp_rat: Detecting a value shortfall quickly after install limits the exposure of this manager's validated investment claim.

rows.source_job: feel confident the funding decision holds up
rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of feeling exposed when leadership re-examines the compounding system capital commitment after the funds are spent
rows.imp_rat: Feeling exposed under post-spend leadership re-examination is a real emotional stake for this capital steward.

rows.source_job: feel confident the funding decision holds up
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of doubt about whether the assumptions behind the mixing system decision will still hold under later scrutiny
rows.imp_rat: Doubt that assumptions will survive later scrutiny weighs on this manager's confidence that the funding decision holds up.

rows.source_job: feel reassured the supplier will deliver
rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of feeling anxious that the selected supplier will fail to meet the compounding unit's requirements once installed
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety that the chosen supplier fails once installed is tied directly to this manager's sourcing accountability in sterile production.

rows.source_job: feel reassured the supplier will deliver
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of unease about whether the mixing system will perform as promised after commissioning
rows.imp_rat: Unease about post-commissioning performance is a genuine emotional load for a manager who staked funding on the mixing system delivering.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a sterility or contamination breach going undetected when confirming operational fit in a sterile fill-finish line
rows.imp_rat: Sterility breach detection is existential in a sterile fill-finish line, but it sits at the edge of the mixing engineer's core job of specifying blend/homogeneity capability rather than at its center.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of unsupported assertion in the documented rationale for the recommended compounding parameters
rows.imp_rat: Unsupported assertions in the documented rationale weaken the scientist's justification job and invite regulatory and reviewer challenge.

rows.source_job: trust the bulk will meet target
rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of surprise concentration failures that erode confidence in the recommended parameters
rows.imp_rat: Surprise concentration failures directly erode confidence in the recommended parameters the scientist stands behind.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in protocol test instructions that leads to inconsistent execution across operators
rows.imp_rat: Ambiguous protocol instructions cause inconsistent execution across operators, threatening data validity in verification runs.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of second-guessing the spec because supporting evidence for homogeneity performance feels incomplete
rows.imp_rat: Second-guessing the spec from incomplete homogeneity evidence saps decisiveness that the engineer's authoring role depends on.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time to assemble the evidence linking each compounding parameter to its homogeneity outcome
rows.imp_rat: Time to assemble evidence linking each parameter to its homogeneity outcome is a productivity concern for the justification job, secondary to getting the science right.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time spent reconciling the qualification dossier with shifting regulatory expectations during review
rows.imp_rat: Reconciling the dossier with shifting expectations during review delays approval, a moderate burden on the compliance job.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of protocol amendments required after a design flaw is found mid-execution
rows.imp_rat: Mid-execution protocol amendments cost time and signal design weakness, but are recoverable and less critical than missed risks.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time required to isolate the root cause of a homogeneity deviation observed during testing
rows.imp_rat: Faster root-cause isolation shortens qualification cycles, but speed matters less than getting the cause right.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time lost to repeating verification runs that were improperly executed
rows.imp_rat: Repeating improperly executed runs wastes schedule and resources, a real but recoverable efficiency loss for the engineer.

rows.stmt: Minimize the number of gaps in the record of actual mixing conditions across the duration of the batch
rows.imp_rat: Complete records of actual mixing conditions are a GMP requirement, and gaps expose the batch to data-integrity findings the technician is accountable for.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of incompleteness in the supporting documentation required to validly authorize the fund release
rows.imp_rat: Incomplete authorization documentation can invalidate or delay the fund release, a direct blocker to executing the capital-authorization job in a GMP environment.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time required for slow-dissolving components to fully enter solution during charging
rows.imp_rat: Slow-dissolving components stretch the charging step and risk incomplete solution, relevant to dissolution but bounded to specific formulations.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of corrective adjustments that fail to bring the batch back on-spec on the first attempt
rows.imp_rat: A corrective adjustment that misses on the first try adds cycle risk and rework, but resolving anomalies is a secondary rather than primary part of charging and blending.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of inconsistency in the operator's batch outcomes as judged by production and quality leadership
rows.imp_rat: Consistency across batches is how production and quality leadership judge the operator, so variability erodes their standing.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time between recognizing an unresolvable deviation and reaching the person who can act on it
rows.imp_rat: Reaching the right decision-maker quickly matters for unresolvable deviations, but this is an occasional exception rather than the daily core task.

rows.stmt: Minimize the percentage of escalations that omit the process context needed to act on the deviation
rows.imp_rat: Escalations lacking process context slow resolution, but crafting the escalation is a secondary task to actually running the mix.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time lost to reaching homogeneity caused by suboptimal initial parameter settings
rows.imp_rat: Time lost to homogeneity from poor initial settings hurts throughput, but a slower correct batch is far preferable to a fast bad one for this technician.

rows.source_job: feel confident the batch is right
rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of second-guessing the endpoint decision based on conflicting in-process readings
rows.imp_rat: Second-guessing the endpoint on conflicting readings undermines confidence, but it is a felt discomfort rather than a direct spec failure.

rows.source_job: feel in control of the batch
rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of uncertainty about whether an adjustment will pull a drifting parameter back within tolerance in time
rows.imp_rat: Feeling in control of whether an adjustment will recover a drifting parameter in time reduces stress, but it is an emotional layer atop the technical job.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time a batch waits because the assigned operator is unavailable at the scheduled start
rows.imp_rat: An operator unavailable at scheduled start idles a tank window and delays the batch, a schedule risk the supervisor manages daily.

rows.source_job: feel confident the batch was done right
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of doubt the overseer carries about whether the batch was compounded exactly per procedure
rows.imp_rat: Carrying doubt about exact procedural conformance undermines the supervisor's confidence in releasing the batch.

rows.source_job: feel confident the batch was done right
rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of the overseer being unsure whether the batch documentation will hold up to review
rows.imp_rat: Doubt about whether documentation will hold up to review weighs on the supervisor whose sign-off closes the batch.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time materials wait in quarantine before conformance to defined acceptance criteria is confirmed
rows.imp_rat: Quarantine dwell time affects throughput more than compliance, so it is a secondary concern to the QA manager's core conformance mandate.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of recommendations that fail to deliver uniform bulk at target across scales, undermining recognized authority
rows.imp_rat: Recommendations that fail to deliver uniform bulk across scales publicly undermine the scientist's recognized expertise.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree to which inconsistent outcomes across input variations erode standing as the definitive compounding authority
rows.imp_rat: Inconsistent outcomes across input variations erode the scientist's claim to definitive authority, a status concern tied to reproducibility.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of revising acceptance criteria after qualification testing has already begun
rows.imp_rat: Revising acceptance criteria after testing starts risks the appearance of criteria-chasing and rework, a moderate concern for defensibility.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time the compounding system capital request spends awaiting authorization in the approval chain
rows.imp_rat: Time the capital request spends in the approval chain delays the whole procurement this manager is accountable for delivering.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time required to identify a sufficient field of qualified suppliers for the specialized compounding system
rows.imp_rat: Time to assemble a sufficient field of qualified suppliers affects schedule for this specialized sourcing job.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of disagreement among budget claimants over the relative merit of this compounding investment
rows.imp_rat: Resolving disagreement among budget claimants smooths the authorization but is a means to the allocation job rather than the director's primary stake.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of redundant testing performed beyond what is needed to reach a confident qualification decision
rows.imp_rat: Reducing redundant testing improves efficiency, but the engineer's priority is confidence over economy, so this is a secondary concern.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time spent confirming a mixing approach operates within cycle-time and changeover constraints on the production line
rows.imp_rat: Confirming cycle-time and changeover fit matters for operational validation but is secondary to whether the mix reaches target homogeneity.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of a qualification conclusion being second-guessed by leadership, peers, or regulators
rows.imp_rat: Being second-guessed is uncomfortable and erodes authority, but is less damaging than an outright overturn.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time required to reach a confirmed homogeneous state after all components are charged
rows.imp_rat: Time to confirmed homogeneity affects cycle efficiency, though reaching homogeneity itself matters more than the speed of doing so.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of concentration drift caused by solvent evaporation during extended hold periods
rows.imp_rat: Solvent evaporation drift can push concentration off target during long holds, relevant but only in extended-hold scenarios.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of staff being uncertain which compounding task they are assigned to at the start of a shift
rows.imp_rat: Staff uncertain of their assigned task at shift start delays the supervisor's ability to launch batches on time.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that formulation and manufacturing teams question the scientific basis of the compounding recommendations
rows.imp_rat: Teams questioning the scientific basis of recommendations threatens the scientist's standing as compounding authority, a real but status-oriented stake.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in which approval thresholds and signatories apply to the capital acquisition of the mixing system
rows.imp_rat: Knowing which approval thresholds and signatories apply is basic to executing a compliant capital acquisition without rework.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of anxiety that the recommended equipment will behave unpredictably once in routine production
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety about unpredictable routine-production behavior is a modest emotional factor next to the objective performance risks the engineer owns.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of worry that the equipment will drift outside production constraints during a run
rows.imp_rat: Worry about equipment drifting outside production constraints is a real but secondary emotional load relative to the engineer's technical deliverables.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of a mixing recommendation being overruled because the rationale is not seen as authoritative
rows.imp_rat: Having a recommendation overruled for weak-seeming rationale threatens the engineer's standing but is a status concern, not the technical job itself.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of times a specified approach must be reversed after the fact in front of stakeholders
rows.imp_rat: Publicly reversing a specified approach damages perceived authority, but it is a downstream status consequence rather than a core-job outcome.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of documentation revisions triggered by reviewer challenges to the formulation rationale
rows.imp_rat: Documentation revisions from reviewer challenges are mostly an efficiency and credibility burden rather than a direct product-quality outcome.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time spent reconstructing missing documentation after testing is complete
rows.imp_rat: Reconstructing missing documentation after testing is wasteful and risky but avoidable with discipline, so moderate stakes.

rows.source_job: feel in control of the batch sequence
rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of surprise the overseer feels when the batch sequence shifts unexpectedly
rows.imp_rat: Feeling in control by avoiding surprise sequence shifts matters for the supervisor's composure but is secondary to functional throughput.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time lost to re-sampling and re-testing before a batch can advance from compounding
rows.imp_rat: Re-sampling and re-testing delays affect cycle time and advancement but not the intrinsic quality of the operator's bulk.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time spent deliberating qualification scope before testing can begin
rows.imp_rat: Time spent deliberating scope is an efficiency concern that the engineer tolerates in favor of getting scope right.

rows.stmt: Minimize the amount of avoidable energy and utility consumption attributable to an oversized or mismatched compounding system
rows.imp_rat: Energy/utility waste is a real but minor line in the TCO of a sterile compounding skid relative to validation and consumables, so it matters modestly to this procurement job.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of idle wait time between sequential compounding steps within a cycle
rows.imp_rat: Idle wait time affects throughput but has little bearing on whether the bulk itself is correctly dissolved, blended and on-concentration.
